title: After I changed the username, podman didn't work.
body:
```
sudo usermod -l admin -d /home/admin -m test

podman --log-level=debug container ls
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called list.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level=debug container ls) 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/etc/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/bin/conmon"              
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/admin/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Overriding graph root "/home/admin/.local/share/containers/storage" with "/home/test/.local/share/containers/storage" from database 
DEBU[0000] Overriding static dir "/home/admin/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" with "/home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod" from database 
DEBU[0000] Overriding volume path "/home/admin/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes" with "/home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes" from database 
DEBU[0000] systemd-logind: Unknown object '/'.          
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/test/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000/containers     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/test/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Cached value indicated that overlay is supported 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
Error: mkdir /home/test: permission denied
```
Should I delete my containers and images now?

Or edit bolt_state.db?

Is there no utility that will solve my dilemma?

comments.body:
Yes easiest to just podman system reset, you could try to edit the database.
Not something we will fix.
@daiaji Have you tried doing `sed` i.e `find and replace` on database. I am not sure if that will work but worth a try.
@flouthoc But unfortunately, the `~/.local/share/containers` folder has been deleted by me.😅
Whoops !!! then `reset` is the only option I guess.
